items.title: NT ambulance service at risk of failure 'all the time' amid under-resourcing

children.content: The Northern Territory’s ambulances service has reached "critical system failure” for the second time in a week, as a St John NT director says the service is so under-resourced its operations are at risk of failure "all the time".

gallery.altText: A St John paramedic dressed in uniform and standing in front of an ambulance, it's centered on a logo emblazoned on their arm.

thumbnail.altText: A St John paramedic dressed in uniform and standing in front of an ambulance, it's centered on a logo emblazoned on their arm.

items.title: NT prosecutors considering appeal of four-year DV manslaughter sentence

children.content: A four-year prison term handed to a man who fatally stabbed in his wife on the Darwin Esplanade in 2023 could be appealed by prosecutors.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: Darwin midwife's $1.1 million government contract under review

children.content: Darwin midwife Claire Marks was under investigation by the nursing and midwifery watchdog when she was handed a $1.1 million contract by the NT government, which is now reviewing the matter, as doctors continue to raise concerns, and the service brings on new staff.

image.altText: A woman in a brown dress looks at the camera.

items.title: Nine weeks of rolling phone, internet outages in remote NT community

children.content: Wadeye, one of Australia's largest Aboriginal communities, has been without a stable mobile phone service for months, leaving locals unable to make calls or access the internet.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a remote NT community, with homes scattered alongside red dirt roads.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a remote NT community, with homes scattered alongside red dirt roads.

items.title: 'A worker is going to be stabbed': Union airs hospital safety concerns

children.content: NT Health has rejected calls for a security review at Royal Darwin Hospital's busy emergency department amid threats against staff, with staff concerned "a worker is going to be stabbed".

image.altText: Staff wearing masks in emergency department at hospital

items.title: Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners

children.content: The Northern Territory government has introduced legislation intended to reform Australia's de facto online gambling regulator, but it's been criticised as a "damage-control exercise".

image.altText: A person holding a mobile phone in their hand, with a sports betting app open on the phone.

items.title: Woman's 'suspicious' death investigated after car allegedly hit by kangaroo

children.content: NT Police are investigating the "suspicious death" of a woman on a remote stretch of highway in the early hours of Easter morning.

image.altText: An empty highway on a remote landscape, with red dirt meeting the edge of the bitumen.

items.title: Vet helping native bat pups has 'never seen mass casualties like this'

children.content: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

image.altText: A volunteer feeds a rescued baby flying fox, which is swaddled in a colourful blanket.

items.title: Artist shares origin story of Field of Light installation at Uluru

children.content: Bruce Munro's  art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

gallery.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
Vibrant reds, oranges and purples cover the ground under a night sky, while in the fore tourists take photos of each other.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene

thumbnail.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
Vibrant reds, oranges and purples cover the ground under a night sky, while in the fore tourists take photos of each other.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to remain in custody on war crimes charges

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith will remain in custody after his lawyers made no application for bail in his first court hearing since his arrest.

image.altText: a man wearing a suit arriving at a court and looking at the camera

items.title: 'Offensive', 'degrading': Court documents reveal Jackie O's complaints

children.content: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

image.altText: A selfie of Jackie O Henderson smiling with Kyle Sandilands in the background wearing radio headphones.

items.title: Police failures exposed during inquest into 50 year cold case

children.content: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of Veronica Green who wears a long white dress.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: Human bones found on Rottnest Island, investigation underway

children.content: Police are investigating the origin of what are believed to be several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

image.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.

items.title: 'The whole industry will fall over': Transport leaders call for urgent action

children.content: An emergency Fair Work Commission hearing in Sydney begins, with unions and employers in the transport industry saying drivers, employees and businesses are on the verge of going under, calling on the top of the supply chain to share the load.

image.altText: A large silver truck drives off into a pink sunset.

items.title: Pet dog survives 'tug-of-war' with crocodile at remote WA lake

children.content: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

items.title: Trump and Vance wade into Hungarian election to save far right Orban

children.content: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

image.altText: Viktor Orbán and JD Vance wave at a political rally in Hungary

items.title: Three-day hearing set for Grace Tame abuser over alleged 'menacing' social posts

children.content: Nicolaas Bester, who was previously found guilty of sexually abusing Grace Tame, has been deemed unfit to stand trial over allegations he made "menacing" posts directed at her on social media.

image.altText: Man exiting sandstone building.

items.title: When is the best time to get your flu vaccine?

children.content: When working out when is the best time to get your vaccine, you might be balancing a theoretical benefit by waiting against a chance of actually getting the flu before you get vaccinated.

gallery.altText: person puts a bandaid on another person who has just had an injection in their arm

thumbnail.altText: person puts a bandaid on another person who has just had an injection in their arm

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?

children.content: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

image.altText: Lady holding a wallet with 20 dollar bills.

items.title: The Handmaid's Tale's sequel arrives with similarly eerie timing

children.content: The Handmaid's Tale sequel, The Testaments, focuses on young women being groomed for marriage at an elite preparatory school headed by Aunt Lydia in Gilead.

image.altText: Aunt Lydia from the back with hair in a stern bun, two young women with hands clasped and in uniform looking at her

items.title: Smallest town in Australia for sale as two residents decide to sell roadhouse

children.content: Cooladdi, nine hours west of Brisbane, dwindled in population when its railway station closed, but it continues to attract road-trippers and graziers. Its owners run local services including a shop, motel and mail service.

gallery.altText: sign reading FOX TRAP beside a road in front of a sunset
a low bright orange building next to a road
two women stand behind a bar in front of fridges, with cowbells hanging above them

two women stand behind a bar in front of fridges, with cowbells hanging above them

items.title: First person charged with 'from the river to the sea' offence faces court

children.content: Liam Parry, 33, was arrested and charged last month for reciting the banned phrase "from the river to the sea."

image.altText: A man speaks in front of several microphones

items.title: Driver seriously hurt in truck crash that shut Melbourne freeway for hours

children.content: The crash shutdown outbound lanes of the Princes Freeway at Officer in Melbourne's south-east.

image.altText: An aerial photo of fire trucks, ambulances and emergency workers surrounding two crashed trucks on a freeway on a sunny day.

items.title: From 'anger and hate' to feeling 'lighter', Jack is back to being herself

children.content: Shayna Jack says her Paris Olympics campaign was fuelled by "anger and hate", but she is back to being herself — lighter, and with more personal belief than ever.

gallery.altText: Shayna Jack cries as she is hugged by her coach Dean Boxall

thumbnail.altText: Shayna Jack cries as she is hugged by her coach Dean Boxall

items.title: Former coach says AFL 'shamed' him over $20K fine

children.content: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

image.altText: Coach celebrates with players, staff, in front of fans, at full-time of an AFL match

items.title: The three reasons behind Penrith's unstoppable start to 2026

children.content: Already, the 2026 premiership looks like it's Penrith's to lose, and there are three big reasons behind the Panthers' record-breaking start to the season.

image.altText: Tom Jenkins celebrates a try in a rugby league match

items.title: Where cattle once roamed, conservation now grows — and so do concerns

children.content: Hailed as a win for the environment, $200 million has been spent purchasing new national parks in outback NSW — but not everyone is celebrating.

gallery.altText: A man leaning on his ute.
A series of dry and water filled channel in outback nsw
A man watches out over the national parks

A series of dry and water filled channel in outback nsw

items.title: Amid 'unprecedented' times, farmers take 'risky' gamble on early winter crops

children.content: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

image.altText: A drone shot of dry seeding in a paddock near Jamestown, in SA's mid north in May 2025

items.title: Farmers angered by plans to turn coal mine into lake bigger than Sydney Harbour

children.content: There are plans to turn the former Hazelwood coal mine in Victoria into a lake bigger than Sydney Harbour, but farmers and environmentalists are worried about the impact on nearby waterways.

image.altText: A young man with a beard, wearing a blue shirt and cap stands alongside a riverbed.

items.title: Reefs at risk as warming water sees sea urchins spread further

children.content: Millions of destructive sea urchins are spreading from NSW into Victoria and Tasmania and the chair of a Senate inquiry says reefs are at risk unless there is decisive action.

image.altText: diver under water with sea urchins

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Cost of filling best-selling car up by $100 since start of Iran war

children.content: The ABC analyses how the cost of a full tank in Australia's most-purchased cars of 2025 has changed since the beginning of the Iran war.

gallery.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

thumbnail.altText: A row of ford utes outside a dealership.

items.title: Parents found guilty of starving daughter win appeal, granted bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

image.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.

items.title: Vehicle rollover on WA mine site kills worker, seriously injures another

children.content: A 38-year-old man dies in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

image.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Why this photo shoot made rugby star Mel Kawa feel like a fraud

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

gallery.altText: Five women's Super Rugby captains pose for a photos with young girls, al with their hands on their hips.

thumbnail.altText: Five women's Super Rugby captains pose for a photos with young girls, al with their hands on their hips.
